sanskrit: विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् । श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥
hindiTranslation: शास्त्रविधिसे हीन, अन्नदानसे रहित, बिना मन्त्रोंके, बिना दक्षिणाके और बिना श्रद्धाके किये जानेवाले यज्ञको तामस यज्ञ कहते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: They call a sacrifice dark when it lacks scriptural rule, food offering, mantra, fee, and trust.
essence: Ritual without trust becomes empty and heavy.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A sacrifice done without proper guidance, offering, mantra, giving, or trust is called dark and lifeless.
simpleInsight: What you do matters less than the sincerity and care you bring to it.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Krishna is classifying the three kinds of sacrifice for Arjuna. After describing pure and driven offerings, he names the lowest form: a ritual performed mechanically, without trust or reverence, which cannot uplift the one who performs it.
modernRelevance: You light a candle, say the words, and still feel nothing because your attention never arrived. A practice done on autopilot may look complete, yet it carries no real force.
translationLiteral: विधिहीनम् (without rule) / असृष्टान्नम् (without offered food) / मन्त्रहीनम् (without mantra) / अदक्षिणम् (without fee) / श्रद्धाविरहितम् (without trust) / यज्ञम् (sacrifice) / तामसम् (dark) / परिचक्षते (they call)
